name: Interval Timeline Overlay UI
purpose: Horizontal interval-workout schedule overlay that complements the current-interval HUD by showing surrounding workout progress.

visualModel.currentSegment: larger, brighter, centered by default, with live progress fill
visualModel.edgeOverflow: low-contrast ··· hints on the left and/or right when hidden segments exist; no WU/CD ghost labels or xN count boxes
visualModel.marker: upward NOW marker in a reserved marker lane below the rail, aligned to live progress or current segment center, without changing rail or segment geometry
visualModel.railDots: optional line-and-dot decoration rail; spacing controls vertical distance from segments, dots control size/color/alpha, and the line controls width/color

labelModel.description: Current distance and current time are independent rows and can be shown together. Non-current segments can hide text or show one selected metric.

layoutModes.description: Keep current lap centered and show a fixed number of neighboring laps.
layoutModes.overflow: hidden previous/next laps are summarized with ellipsis hints

layoutModes.description: Render the complete workout sequence across the available width.
layoutModes.overflow: labels are reduced or hidden when blocks become too narrow

layoutModes.description: Compress repeated work/rest pairs into a set rail while expanding the current pair.

readabilityRules: Default mode is centeredWindow for high-repetition readability.
Full Schedule always shows all enabled segments and never automatically falls back to centeredWindow.
Full Schedule supports equal-width and duration-proportional segment layout.
Full Schedule Equal layout uses equal widths at the Current Width minimum and can enlarge the current segment as the slider increases.
WU, Rest, and CD visibility toggles filter segment kinds before layout.
Current segment remains visually centered in centeredWindow mode.
Labels are configured directly: current distance and current time can be hidden or set to live/remaining, while non-current labels can be hidden or set to distance/time.
For high-repeat workouts such as 1min x25, show nearby context and summarize hidden repetitions at both edges.
Marker toggling must not move the rail, marker lane, or segment layout.
Marker triangle and label must stay inside the background and border.
Overflow hints use compact ellipsis-only edge space and do not render WU/CD endpoint labels.
Rail spacing expands rendered background height so the rail remains inside the background.
Overlay bounds must remain stable when current lap changes.
